tool.name: Recount3
tool.description: Retrieve harmonized gene expression from TCGA and GTEx

configfiles.configfile:
suppressPackageStartupMessages({ library(recount3) library(recount) library(SummarizedExperiment) })
## ── 0. Parse arguments ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)
data_source <- args[1] tissue_type <- args[2] output_raw <- args[3] output_tpm <- args[4] # "NULL" when normalize = FALSE output_annotation <- args[5] output_gene_annot <- args[6] normalize <- as.logical(args[7])
cat(" recount3 tool is starting!\n") cat(" data_source :", data_source, "\n") cat(" tissue_type :", tissue_type, "\n") cat(" normalize :", normalize, "\n")
## Extend download timeout
options(timeout = 600)
## Load the project table and find the requested project ## project_table.tsv is shipped with the tool and copied to the working ## directory by the <command> block before this script runs.
cat("Step 1: Reading project table...\n") project_table <- read.delim("project_table.tsv", row.names = 1)
## Find the requested project ## Filter only on the project name column — do NOT filter on project_type ## because that column name differs across project_table.tsv versions.
project_info <- subset(project_table, project == data_source)
## Detect source database is_tcga <- grepl("tcga", project_info$file_source, ignore.case = TRUE) is_gtex <- grepl("gtex", project_info$file_source, ignore.case = TRUE)
cat("Step 1 done: source =", project_info$file_source, "| n_samples =", project_info$n_samples, "\n")
## Download RSE
cat("Step 2: Downloading RSE from recount3 servers...\n")
rse <- recount3::create_rse( project_info, type = "gene", annotation = "gencode_v29" )
if (is.null(rse) || ncol(rse) == 0) { cat("ERROR: create_rse() returned an empty object for project:", data_source, "\n") quit(status = 1) }
cat("Step 2 done:", nrow(rse), "genes x", ncol(rse), "samples\n")
## Convert coverage to integer counts ## recount3 stores base-level coverage, not read counts. ## transform_counts() converts to integer counts comparable to ## featureCounts / HTSeq output — required by DESeq2, edgeR, limma-voom.
cat("Step 3: Converting coverage to integer counts...\n") assay(rse, "counts") <- recount3::transform_counts(rse)
## Tissue-type filtering ## ## TCGA: may contain both tumour and adjacent samples in one project. ## Filter when tissue_type != "both". ## ## GTEx: all samples are normal tissue — no tumour/normal distinction. ## Skip filtering entirely; tissue_type param is silently ignored.
if (is_tcga && tissue_type != "both") {
cat("Step 4: Applying tissue-type filter '", tissue_type, "'...\n", sep = "") coldata <- as.data.frame(colData(rse))
## Find sample-type column — name varies across TCGA projects sample_type_col <- NULL for (candidate in c("tcga.cgc_sample_sample_type", "sample_type", "sample_type.samples", "tcga_tissue_type")) { if (candidate %in% colnames(coldata)) { sample_type_col <- candidate break } }
if (is.null(sample_type_col)) { ## Column not found — warn and return all samples unfiltered cat("WARNING: No sample-type column found — skipping filter, returning all samples.\n") cat(" Available colData columns:", paste(colnames(coldata), collapse = ", "), "\n") } else { cat(" Using column:", sample_type_col, "\n") sample_types <- as.character(coldata[[sample_type_col]])
## TCGA labels contain substrings "Tumor" or "normal" if (tissue_type == "tumor") { keep <- grepl("tumor", sample_types, ignore.case = TRUE) } else { keep <- grepl("normal", sample_types, ignore.case = TRUE) }
rse <- rse[, keep, drop = FALSE]
if (ncol(rse) == 0) { cat("ERROR: No", tissue_type, "samples found for project:", data_source, "\n") quit(status = 1) } cat(" Retained", ncol(rse), tissue_type, "samples.\n") }
} else if (is_gtex) { cat("Step 4: GTEx project — all", ncol(rse), "samples are normal tissue; tissue-type filter skipped.\n") } else { cat("Step 4: tissue_type = 'both' — no filtering applied.\n") }
## Extract data frames and write outputs
raw_counts <- as.data.frame(assay(rse, "counts")) sample_meta <- as.data.frame(colData(rse)) gene_annot <- as.data.frame(rowData(rse))
## Provides us with raw count matrix ## ALWAYS produced. ## These are the correct input for differential expression tools like DESeq2, edgeR. ## Do NOT pre-normalise before supplying to these tools.
cat("Step 5: Writing raw count matrix →", output_raw, "\n") write.table( raw_counts, file = output_raw, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE, col.names = NA )
## Optionally will provide with TPM-normalised matrix ## Produced ONLY when normalize = TRUE. ## Use TPM for WGCNA co-expression analysis or visualisation. ## Do NOT use TPM as input to differential expression tools.
if (normalize) { cat("Step 6: Computing TPM values via recount::getTPM()...\n") tpm_values <- recount::getTPM(rse) tpm_df <- as.data.frame(tpm_values)
cat(" Writing TPM matrix →", output_tpm, "\n") write.table( tpm_df, file = output_tpm, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE, col.names = NA ) } else { cat("Step 6: normalize = FALSE — TPM matrix not produced.\n") }
## Provides us with sample annotation
cat("Step 7: Writing sample annotation →", output_annotation, "\n") write.table( sample_meta, file = output_annotation, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE, col.names = NA )
## Provides us with gene annotation
cat("Step 8: Writing gene annotation →", output_gene_annot, "\n") write.table( gene_annot, file = output_gene_annot, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE, col.names = NA )
cat(" Raw counts :", output_raw, "\n") if (normalize) cat(" TPM matrix :", output_tpm, "\n") cat(" Sample annotation :", output_annotation, "\n") cat(" Gene annotation :", output_gene_annot, "\n")

param.name: data_source
param.label: Data source
param.help: Select a TCGA cancer type or a GTEx normal tissue.
param.option: TCGA - Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma (HNSC)
TCGA - Kidney Renal Clear Cell Carcinoma (KIRC)
TCGA - Kidney Renal Papillary Cell Carcinoma (KIRP)

param.name: tissue_type
param.label: Tissue type (TCGA only — ignored for GTEx)
param.help: Restrict TCGA samples to tumour, normal, or both. Has no effect on GTEx.

param.name: normalize
param.label: Also output TPM-normalised counts?
param.help: Default: OFF — raw integer counts are always produced and are required for differential expression analysis. Enable only if you additionally need TPM values for WGCNA or visualisation.

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**What it does**
Retrieves harmonised RNA-seq gene-expression data from two major public databases using the Bioconductor **recount3** package:
- **TCGA** (The Cancer Genome Atlas) — 33 cancer types, tumour and/or matched-normal samples - **GTEx** (Genotype-Tissue Expression) — 31 normal human tissues (all non-diseased)
No FASTQ download, alignment, or quantification step required.
----
**Inputs**
Data source : TCGA cancer type or GTEx tissue. Tissue type: For TCGA, restrict to tumour, normal, or both. For GTEx, all samples are normal tissue — this parameter is ignored. Also output TPM-normalised counts?: Default is turned off but can be enabled for WGCNA or visualisation only.
----
**Outputs**
Raw counts matrix (always if not specified): will provide with Integer counts. TPM-normalised matrix (optional): will provide us with Transcripts Per Million. Only when normalisation is enabled. Sample annotation: will provide clinical/technical metadata from colData(rse). Gene annotation: will provide gene symbols and coordinates from rowData(rse).
.. class:: warningmark
**Do not supply TPM values to DESeq2, edgeR, or limma-voom.** These tools require raw integer counts and perform their own internal normalisation.
----
